matches.match_rationale: The absence of a standardized evaluation form aligned with DepEd RPMS is a content generation problem — AI can draft standardized rubric templates, behavioral anchors, and scoring descriptors that align with RPMS competency domains. This directly addresses the ad-hoc/outdated-template problem by producing a canonical, consistent document that all principals can use. Education operators have the RPMS framework published as a reference corpus, giving the model grounded source material.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An AI tool that ingests the official DepEd RPMS framework and generates a complete, campus-agnostic performance evaluation form with competency indicators, rating scales, and narrative guide language — ready for HR to review and deploy across all sites.

matches.match_rationale: Multiple campuses already have ad-hoc checklists and paper templates — these are semi-structured inputs that need to be mapped to a unified target schema (the RPMS framework). AI-assisted schema mapping can normalize the existing heterogeneous criteria into a single standardized structure, revealing which campuses already partially comply and which need the most remediation. This tackles the root cause of inconsistency rather than just producing a new document.
matches.opportunity_sketch: Ingest scanned or digitized evaluation forms from each campus, extract the criteria used, and map them to RPMS competency codes — producing a gap report showing where each campus deviates and a reconciled master schema.

matches.match_rationale: Once a standard rubric exists, AI can classify completed evaluations or written comments by RPMS competency domain, detecting which areas principals are and are not assessing — surfacing compliance gaps and bias patterns across campuses. This addresses the grievance and fairness dimension by providing objective evidence that certain campuses over-index on attendance vs. pedagogy, making audit findings defensible.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A classification layer that processes submitted evaluation narratives and tags each statement to an RPMS competency, then generates a cross-campus compliance dashboard showing coverage gaps and outlier campuses for HR review.

matches.match_rationale: The full evaluation cycle — distributing forms, collecting submissions, checking for completeness, flagging non-compliant rubrics, routing for principal sign-off, and archiving — is a multi-step workflow that today requires manual HR coordination across campuses. A task automation agent can own this pipeline end-to-end, enforcing the standardized rubric at the point of submission and blocking incomplete or off-template evaluations before they create grievances downstream.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An automated evaluation workflow agent that sends the standardized RPMS-aligned form to each campus on schedule, validates submissions against required criteria, flags deviations for HR review, and archives completed evaluations in a central record — reducing manual coordination and ensuring audit-ready compliance.
